records.text: Project: Mega Business Personal Assistant

records.title: Project Name Mega Business Personal Assistant
records.text:
Pod Composition 2 AI Automation Interns + 1 Web Development Intern
Phase Phase 1 — Local / Free-tier Build

records.title: Deadline To be communicated by AutoNova
records.text:
Primary Interface Telegram (voice notes, messages, documents)
1. Background & Context
AutoNova's client operates a real estate business that currently uses AI tools in a fragmented,
manual way — pasting prompts into ChatGPT for document drafting, running calculations separately,
and handling internal operations without any centralised system. The goal of this project is to replace
all of that with a single, self-contained Mega Business Personal Assistant.
The assistant must be secure — it will handle sensitive business data, so it must either run on a local
LLM or on a privacy-first hosted solution with no data leakage. The client controls everything from
Telegram by sending a voice note, a text message, or uploading a document.
2. Project Objectives

records.title: Build a fully functional Telegram-controlled AI assistant that
records.text:
• Knows everything about the client's real estate business (properties, contacts, documents,
pricing, internal SOPs).
• Responds intelligently to voice notes, text messages, and uploaded documents via Telegram.
• Can draft, edit, and modify documents on command — including Excel/spreadsheet
operations.
• Generates images on demand (property mock-ups, visuals, marketing assets).
• Researches and interacts with Indian real estate platforms (99acres, MagicBricks, etc.) to
post listings or retrieve data where APIs are available.
• Handles all routine in-house operations: calculations, report drafting, data lookups, reminders,
and task tracking.
• Keeps all data secure — no third-party training on client data; local LLM preferred or secure

records.title: API with strict data controls.
records.text:
3. Scope of Work
3.1  Core Capabilities (Must Have — Phase 1)
• Telegram Bot Interface
– Accept text messages, voice notes (transcribed to text via Whisper or equivalent), and file uploads.
– Return responses as text, formatted documents, or images.
• Business Knowledge Base
– Ingest and store business-specific information: property listings, contacts, pricing, SOPs, FAQs.
– The assistant must answer questions using this knowledge base first before any general LLM
knowledge.
• Document Operations
– Draft, modify, and format Word/PDF documents on instruction.
– Edit spreadsheets: update values, mark columns, apply formulas — via Telegram upload +
instruction.
– Return the modified file back to the user through Telegram.
• Image Generation
– Generate images from text prompts (property visuals, banners, etc.) using a free-tier image
generation API.
• Secure LLM Backend
– Phase 1: Use a local LLM (e.g. Ollama + LLaMA 3 / Mistral) or OpenRouter with a privacy-safe
model.
– No client data should be used for training or stored by any third-party service.
3.2  Research Tasks (Must Include in Phase 1 Report)
• Investigate the APIs of 99acres, MagicBricks, Housing.com, and NoBroker.
• Document which platforms offer public/private APIs, what actions are possible (post listing,
fetch leads, etc.), and any rate limits or costs.
• Recommend the best integration path for Phase 2 production.
3.3  Web Dashboard (Web Dev Intern)
The Web Dev intern must consult both AI Automation interns before building anything. The

records.title: dashboard must support — not duplicate — the Telegram interface. Suggested scope
records.text:
• Admin panel to view conversation logs and assistant activity.
• Knowledge base manager — upload, update, or remove business documents that the
assistant uses.
• Integration status monitor — show which APIs/services are connected and their health.
• Basic analytics — queries handled per day, document edits, image generations.
No feature should be built without confirming with the AI Automation team that it connects to the
backend.
4. Suggested Tech Stack (Phase 1 — Free / Open Source)

records.title: Layer Recommended Tool / Option
records.text: LLM Backend Ollama (local) with LLaMA 3 or Mistral; or OpenRouter free tier

records.title: Image Generation Stable Diffusion (local) or Pollinations.ai (free)
records.text:
Document Editing python-docx, openpyxl, or LibreOffice headless
Knowledge Base / RAG LlamaIndex or LangChain + ChromaDB (local vector store)
Dashboard (Web Dev) React + Tailwind or Next.js; simple REST backend

records.title: Orchestration
records.text:
n8n (self-hosted, free) or custom Python scripts
All tools used in Phase 1 must be free or open-source. Document every tool chosen and why — this
directly informs the Phase 2 production stack decision.
5. Expected Deliverables

records.title: By the deadline, the pod must submit
records.text:
1. Working Telegram bot connected to a secure LLM with the full feature set described in
Section 3.
2. Populated knowledge base with at least 10–15 sample business entries (properties, contacts,

records.title: SOPs).
records.text:
3. Functional web dashboard as described in Section 3.3.
4. Platform API Research Report (99acres, MagicBricks, etc.) with integration recommendations
for Phase 2.
5. Phase 2 Cost & Stack Recommendation document — best production options with pricing
and trade-offs.
6. GitHub repository with clean, documented code and a README covering setup and usage.
6. Team Structure & Collaboration Rules
6.1  Role Clarity

records.title: AI Automation Interns
records.text:
(×2)
Own the entire automation stack — LLM integration, Telegram bot, RAG
pipeline, document processing, image generation, and API research.
Both must work together; no solo silos.
Web Dev Intern (×1) Build the web dashboard. Must first consult with both AI Automation
interns and agree on API contracts before writing a single line of frontend
code. The dashboard must be genuinely useful, not decorative.
6.2  Collaboration Requirements
• The entire pod must align on architecture, feature scope, and timelines on Day 1 before
anyone starts building.
• The Web Dev intern must not build any feature the AI Automation team has not confirmed
they can support from the backend.
• Use a shared project tracker (Notion, Trello, or GitHub Projects) to track tasks, blockers, and
progress — visible to AutoNova at all times.
• Questions for AutoNova must be compiled and submitted as a single batch (not one-off
messages). AutoNova will respond during designated check-in windows.
7. End-of-Day (EOD) Update Policy
This is non-negotiable. It will not be requested daily — it is your responsibility.
At the end of every working day, at least one member of the pod must post an EOD update

records.title: to the designated AutoNova channel. The update must cover
records.text:
• What was completed today (per team member).
• What is in progress and the expected completion.
• Any blockers — technical, resource, or decision-based.
• Plan for tomorrow.
Failure to submit EOD updates consistently will be treated as a signal of disengagement and
will directly affect the pod's performance rating.
8. Working Norms — No Hand-Holding
AutoNova's team is focused on client work. You will not receive step-by-step guidance. This is
intentional.
• You are expected to research, make decisions, and build independently.
• If you are stuck, exhaust available resources (documentation, community forums, your pod
teammates) before escalating to AutoNova.
• AutoNova will not attend check-in calls unless explicitly scheduled for a specific reason.
• Questions should be meaningful — not things you can find in a 5-minute search.
This is how real-world product teams operate. Treat this like a client project, not a university
assignment.
9. Performance Track
🏆  AutoNova Performance Track
You are one of several pods working on live projects at AutoNova. The number of pods is not
disclosed — what matters is your output.

records.title: How it works
records.text:
1. Pods that consistently meet deadlines and deliver quality work unlock progressively bigger
and more interesting projects.
2. Top-performing pods are first in line to be considered for a Pre-Placement Offer (PPO) from

records.title: AutoNova.
records.text:
3. High performers may be assigned to real client-facing projects before their internship ends.
There are no shortcuts. Impressive, consistent work is the only currency that matters here.
10. Phase 2 — Path to Production
If AutoNova is satisfied with the Phase 1 output, the project moves to Phase 2. At that point:
• The stack will be reviewed and potentially upgraded to paid/production-grade services.
• Real client data will be onboarded into the knowledge base.
• Live integrations with 99acres, MagicBricks, and other platforms will be implemented.
• The Telegram bot will be handed over to the end client for real use.
Phase 2 investment decisions will be based entirely on Phase 1 quality. Build it like it matters —
because it does.
AutoNova AI Automation Agency  |  Intern Pod Programme  |  Confidential — Do Not Distribute
AutoNova — Intern Pod Assignment Pod Problem Statement Project: Mega Business Personal Assistant

Project Overview Client Real Estate Business (AutoNova Client) Project Name Mega Business Personal Assistant Pod Composition 2 AI Automation Interns + 1 Web Development Intern Phase Phase 1 — Local / Free-tier Build Deadline To be communicated by AutoNova Primary Interface Telegram (voice notes, messages, documents)

1. Background & Context AutoNova's client operates a real estate business that currently uses AI tools in a fragmented, manual way — pasting prompts into ChatGPT for document drafting, running calculations separately, and handling internal operations without any centralised system. The goal of this project is to replace all of that with a single, self-contained Mega Business Personal Assistant.

The assistant must be secure — it will handle sensitive business data, so it must either run on a local LLM or on a privacy-first hosted solution with no data leakage. The client controls everything from Telegram by sending a voice note, a text message, or uploading a document.

2. Project Objectives Build a fully functional Telegram-controlled AI assistant that: • Knows everything about the client's real estate business (properties, contacts, documents, pricing, internal SOPs). • Responds intelligently to voice notes, text messages, and uploaded documents via Telegram. • Can draft, edit, and modify documents on command — including Excel/spreadsheet operations. • Generates images on demand (property mock-ups, visuals, marketing assets). • Researches and interacts with Indian real estate platforms (99acres, MagicBricks, etc.) to post listings or retrieve data where APIs are available. • Handles all routine in-house operations: calculations, report drafting, data lookups, reminders, and task tracking. • Keeps all data secure — no third-party training on client data; local LLM preferred or secure API with strict data controls.

3. Scope of Work 3.1 Core Capabilities (Must Have — Phase 1) • Telegram Bot Interface – Accept text messages, voice notes (transcribed to text via Whisper or equivalent), and file uploads. – Return responses as text, formatted documents, or images. • Business Knowledge Base – Ingest and store business-specific information: property listings, contacts, pricing, SOPs, FAQs. – The assistant must answer questions using this knowledge base first before any general LLM knowledge. • Document Operations – Draft, modify, and format Word/PDF documents on instruction. – Edit spreadsheets: update values, mark columns, apply formulas — via Telegram upload + instruction. – Return the modified file back to the user through Telegram. • Image Generation – Generate images from text prompts (property visuals, banners, etc.) using a free-tier image generation API. • Secure LLM Backend – Phase 1: Use a local LLM (e.g. Ollama + LLaMA 3 / Mistral) or OpenRouter with a privacy-safe model. – No client data should be used for training or stored by any third-party service.

3.2 Research Tasks (Must Include in Phase 1 Report) • Investigate the APIs of 99acres, MagicBricks, Housing.com, and NoBroker. • Document which platforms offer public/private APIs, what actions are possible (post listing, fetch leads, etc.), and any rate limits or costs. • Recommend the best integration path for Phase 2 production.

3.3 Web Dashboard (Web Dev Intern) The Web Dev intern must consult both AI Automation interns before building anything. The dashboard must support — not duplicate — the Telegram interface. Suggested scope: • Admin panel to view conversation logs and assistant activity. • Knowledge base manager — upload, update, or remove business documents that the assistant uses. • Integration status monitor — show which APIs/services are connected and their health. • Basic analytics — queries handled per day, document edits, image generations. No feature should be built without confirming with the AI Automation team that it connects to the backend.

4. Suggested Tech Stack (Phase 1 — Free / Open Source) Layer Recommended Tool / Option LLM Backend Ollama (local) with LLaMA 3 or Mistral; or OpenRouter free tier Telegram Bot python-telegram-bot library or Node.js telegraf Voice Transcription OpenAI Whisper (local) or free Whisper API Image Generation Stable Diffusion (local) or Pollinations.ai (free) Document Editing python-docx, openpyxl, or LibreOffice headless Knowledge Base / RAG LlamaIndex or LangChain + ChromaDB (local vector store) Dashboard (Web Dev) React + Tailwind or Next.js; simple REST backend Automation / Orchestration n8n (self-hosted, free) or custom Python scripts
All tools used in Phase 1 must be free or open-source. Document every tool chosen and why — this directly informs the Phase 2 production stack decision.

5. Expected Deliverables By the deadline, the pod must submit: 1. Working Telegram bot connected to a secure LLM with the full feature set described in Section 3. 2. Populated knowledge base with at least 10–15 sample business entries (properties, contacts, SOPs). 3. Functional web dashboard as described in Section 3.3. 4. Platform API Research Report (99acres, MagicBricks, etc.) with integration recommendations for Phase 2. 5. Phase 2 Cost & Stack Recommendation document — best production options with pricing and trade-offs. 6. GitHub repository with clean, documented code and a README covering setup and usage.

6. Team Structure & Collaboration Rules 6.1 Role Clarity Role Responsibility AI Automation Interns (×2) Own the entire automation stack — LLM integration, Telegram bot, RAG pipeline, document processing, image generation, and API research. Both must work together; no solo silos. Web Dev Intern (×1) Build the web dashboard. Must first consult with both AI Automation interns and agree on API contracts before writing a single line of frontend code. The dashboard must be genuinely useful, not decorative.

6.2 Collaboration Requirements • The entire pod must align on architecture, feature scope, and timelines on Day 1 before anyone starts building. • The Web Dev intern must not build any feature the AI Automation team has not confirmed they can support from the backend. • Use a shared project tracker (Notion, Trello, or GitHub Projects) to track tasks, blockers, and progress — visible to AutoNova at all times. • Questions for AutoNova must be compiled and submitted as a single batch (not one-off messages). AutoNova will respond during designated check-in windows.
7. End-of-Day (EOD) Update Policy This is non-negotiable. It will not be requested daily — it is your responsibility.

At the end of every working day, at least one member of the pod must post an EOD update to the designated AutoNova channel. The update must cover: • What was completed today (per team member). • What is in progress and the expected completion. • Any blockers — technical, resource, or decision-based. • Plan for tomorrow.
Failure to submit EOD updates consistently will be treated as a signal of disengagement and will directly affect the pod's performance rating.

8. Working Norms — No Hand-Holding AutoNova's team is focused on client work. You will not receive step-by-step guidance. This is intentional. • You are expected to research, make decisions, and build independently. • If you are stuck, exhaust available resources (documentation, community forums, your pod teammates) before escalating to AutoNova. • AutoNova will not attend check-in calls unless explicitly scheduled for a specific reason. • Questions should be meaningful — not things you can find in a 5-minute search.
This is how real-world product teams operate. Treat this like a client project, not a university assignment.
9. Performance Track 🏆 AutoNova Performance Track
You are one of several pods working on live projects at AutoNova. The number of pods is not disclosed — what matters is your output.

How it works: 1. Pods that consistently meet deadlines and deliver quality work unlock progressively bigger and more interesting projects. 2. Top-performing pods are first in line to be considered for a Pre-Placement Offer (PPO) from AutoNova. 3. High performers may be assigned to real client-facing projects before their internship ends.
There are no shortcuts. Impressive, consistent work is the only currency that matters here.

10. Phase 2 — Path to Production If AutoNova is satisfied with the Phase 1 output, the project moves to Phase 2. At that point: • The stack will be reviewed and potentially upgraded to paid/production-grade services. • Real client data will be onboarded into the knowledge base. • Live integrations with 99acres, MagicBricks, and other platforms will be implemented. • The Telegram bot will be handed over to the end client for real use.
Phase 2 investment decisions will be based entirely on Phase 1 quality. Build it like it matters — because it does.
